behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error (validator -> reject path -> DLQ payload) where row-number metadata can be lost or shifted. Required setup: run CSV parsing + validation + DLQ writer together with an in-process DLQ sink and assert rejected record includes original CSV row index. Integration is the minimum because this is not just one function’s logic; it is cross-module routing. Higher levels rejected: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess, and it has no AI/ML output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a small CSV fixture with known missing required fields through the pipeline components wired together; assert valid rows continue, invalid rows are emitted to DLQ, and each DLQ message includes the exact original row number from the source file.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: logic/parsing error in date normalization rules. Required setup: direct calls to the date transformation function with representative inputs. Unit is minimum because the behavior targets deterministic conversion logic, not inter-module wiring. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is strictly about format conversion and contains no required OS/network/subprocess or AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for the transformer: pass ISO 8601, US, and European date strings (including edge cases like ambiguous day/month and invalid dates) and assert canonical output or explicit validation error.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/runtime resource failure (memory pressure, GC behavior, wall-clock timeout) that appears under real process execution at scale. Required setup: execute the pipeline as a real process with a large input and enforce timeout/memory observation. System is minimum because in-process unit/integration fakes can hide real OOM and timing behavior. Higher levels rejected: behavior text has no AI judgment or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end load test with a generated 500,000-row CSV under production-like runtime settings; measure peak RSS and total duration, and fail if timeout is exceeded or process crashes/gets OOM-killed.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: external-system error handling mismatch (real HTTP/S3 503 semantics to retry policy), including backoff timing behavior. Required setup: real networked S3-compatible endpoint/proxy that returns transient 503, then success, while observing retry intervals and batch continuation. System is minimum because mocking client calls can miss real SDK/HTTP error translation and retry triggers. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is deterministic resilience logic, not AI/ML or multi-agent orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline against an S3-compatible test endpoint configured to return 503 for initial attempts and success afterward; assert exponential retry intervals and verify the batch completes instead of aborting.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: restart/recovery consistency error across process boundary (checkpoint/commit ordering, idempotency) causing duplicate inserts after crash/restart. Required setup: real PostgreSQL plus actual pipeline stop/restart during batch processing, then verification of uniqueness/count invariants. System is minimum because restart semantics and persistent state interaction are process/DB-environment behaviors that mocks often hide. Higher levels rejected: behavior text describes deterministic persistence semantics, not AI/ML output quality or multi-agent pipeline steps.
behaviors.test_description: Start pipeline on a batch, force termination mid-run, restart with same state, and assert PostgreSQL contains each logical row exactly once (via unique keys/count checks) while remaining rows complete.
